calendar.matters:
Petition For Approval Of Guardian Ad Litem Fees And For Allowance Of Fees And Costs Advanced To Attorneys Of Guardian Ad Litem
Off Calendar Dec-05-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Melanie Emmons for Guardian ad Litem Hong Chew, who was also present.

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Approval of Guardian ad Litem Fees.

The Court stated that it had reviewed the recent declaration regarding the size of the trust estate. The Court asked whether there were any objections to the petition. No objections were made.

There being no objection, the Court took the matter under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account; For Removal Of Trustee; For Appointment Of Successor Trustee; For Waiver Of Bondl And To Reimburse Dependency A
Off Calendar Oct-24-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Rowena Navia for Hong Chew and Barbara Simon.

Ms. Navia stated that she thought that Ms. D'Amato would appear. The Court addressed the injunction regarding the ex parte for the appointment of a guardian ad litem, that there was no order in the file, but that the ex parte was granted subject to a petition for guardian ad litem. Ms. Navia stated that it was filed. The Court stated that there was no order, that it believes that it is a mandatory judicial council order, that it appears that Mr. Chew was appointed, and that it had received Ms. Simons consent to serve. Mr. Chew addressed the fees stating that they are not seeking any fees from this trust, that Kimiko Burton is not seeking any fees from this trust for the custody or care of the children, and that he was only asking for his fees as guardian ad litem. Ms. Navia stated that there would be legal fees. The Court noted that it had no background re: the fees. It was noted that the fees would be $128.00 an hour - at the juvenile dependency rate. Ms. Navia stated that they would have Mr. Chew petition for fees. The Court stated that it would like to see a declaration regarding services, that it would be a regularly filed petition, and that there should be a petition by the petitioner and guardian ad litem in connection with the request for fees. Ms. Navia stated that they would be filing for authority with regard to several other proceedings. Mr. Chew stated that there would need to be an eviction. Ms. Navia stated that there would need to be counsel in place to assist Ms. Simon. The Court stated that the petition appeared to be okay to grant and that it would want an accounting from Ms. D'Amato from 8/11/14 to the present. Ms. Simon stated that Ms. D'Amato never disclosed to her that she never had the children, that Ms. D'Amato had normal bills with her such as PG&E (etc.), and that Ms. D'Amato may likely bring in a box of papers & say here is the accounting. Ms. Simon stated that she was providing information and was not dissuading the Court. The Court stated that it could be addressed at a later time. Mr. Chew then presented an update for the Court and stated that he wants to sell the property. Ms. Navia stated that she did not have a chance to look through the trust. Brief discussions regarding bringing the trust under Court supervision are held. Ms. Navia stated that if they need to have authority to sell the property then they would. Ms. Hilton stated that the trust could be brought under Court supervision for one year. The Court stated that there was nothing to bond at this time and requested that counsel look a an order appointing the guardian ad litem. Ms. Navia stated that they would prepare another order for Judge Busch's signature. The Court then took the Pettion to Compel Trustee to Account; for Removal of Trustee; for Appointment of Successor Trustee; for Waiver of Bond; and to Reimburse Dependency Attorney Fees Pursuant to WIC Sections 903.1(1)(a) matter taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Probate Examiner: Probate Director Sandy Hilton. Deputy: E. Lopes. Matter not reported.
